data.title: USA Rare Earth Announces Board Changes Ahead of Merger
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade USAR

USA Rare Earth ( (USAR) ) has shared an update.

On April 20, 2026, USA Rare Earth, Inc. announced that directors Mordechai Gutnick and General Paul Kern would not stand for reelection at the June 3, 2026 annual shareholders’ meeting, prompting the board to reduce its size to six members ahead of that date. The company said their decisions were unrelated to any disagreements over operations or strategy and thanked both for their years of service.

The governance changes are tied to USA Rare Earth’s April 19, 2026 merger agreement under which SVRE Holdings Ltd. will merge into a subsidiary of the company as part of its acquisition of Serra Verde Rare Earths Ltd., after which two Serra Verde nominees will join the board and expand it to eight seats. The acquisition, expected to close in the third quarter of 2026 subject to regulatory approvals and other conditions, is set to reshape the board’s composition and may strengthen the company’s strategic position in the rare earths market.

The most recent analyst rating on (USAR) stock is a Buy with a $45.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on USA Rare Earth stock, see the USAR Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on USAR Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, USAR is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by weak financial performance (2025 revenue collapse, very large losses, and consistently negative free cash flow). Offsetting this, technicals are positive with the stock above key moving averages, and sentiment/catalysts are supportive due to reported liquidity and multiple strategic transactions—though valuation remains challenged and execution/dilution risks are significant.

To see Spark’s full report on USAR stock, click here.

More about USA Rare Earth

USA Rare Earth, Inc. operates in the rare earths sector, focusing on the development and production of critical rare earth materials that are vital for advanced manufacturing, clean energy technologies, and defense applications. The company targets strategic supply chains that aim to reduce dependence on foreign sources of rare earth elements and strengthen domestic and allied industrial capabilities.

Average Trading Volume: 19,441,280

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $4.98B

For a thorough assessment of USAR stock, go to TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

The latest update is out from Investcorp Credit Management BDC ( (ICMB) ).

For the quarter ended Dec. 31, 2025, Investcorp Credit Management BDC reported a portfolio with a total fair market value of $172.7 million across 67 debt and equity positions in 37 companies and 18 industries. Senior secured first-lien debt made up about 81% of the portfolio, with a weighted-average coupon of 9.1%, net leverage of 5.2x, interest coverage of 2.2x and a portfolio loan-to-value of 46.3%.

The top 10 holdings accounted for roughly $85.3 million in fair value, led by investments such as Bioplan, WorkGenius, and Klein Hersh, and the portfolio’s weighted-average fair market value per position was $5.5 million. About 90% of the portfolio’s fair value was rated in the middle of the firm’s five-point risk scale, indicating performance at or slightly below expectations, while non-accruals stood at 6.9% and approximately 20% of fair value carried a payment-in-kind component, underscoring a moderate but manageable risk profile for investors as of year-end 2025.

Spark’s Take on ICMB Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, ICMB is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by sharply deteriorating TTM financial performance (large revenue drop and deep losses) alongside elevated leverage and negative returns. Eagle Financial Services ( (EFSI) ) has provided an announcement.

Eagle Financial Services reported first-quarter 2026 net income of $3.7 million, down from $4.3 million in the fourth quarter of 2025 but sharply improved from a $7.0 million net loss in the first quarter of 2025, which had been affected by securities losses tied to balance sheet repositioning. Earnings per share were $0.69, returns on average equity and assets were 7.98% and 0.81% respectively, and the board declared a quarterly dividend of $0.31 per share on April 23, 2026.

The bank’s net interest margin edged up to 3.63% from 3.61% in the prior quarter, while the efficiency ratio improved to 67.97% from 70.39%, reflecting stronger core operating performance and cost discipline. Management highlighted higher net interest income, improved gain on loan sales and stable asset quality as key drivers of year-over-year improvement versus the adjusted first quarter of 2025, underscoring the positive impact of prior balance sheet actions on profitability and positioning the bank to support customers amid a cautious economic backdrop.

Summary

Bank of Clarke operates within a competitive regional banking landscape, where net interest margin management, efficiency improvements and balance sheet positioning are key drivers of performance. data.title: Vaxart Adds Veteran Biotech Leader to Board
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Vaxart ( (VXRT) ).

On April 23, 2026, Vaxart, Inc., a clinical-stage biotechnology company developing oral vaccines using a proprietary pill-based delivery platform, said it had appointed veteran biotech executive James B. Breitmeyer, M.D., Ph.D., to its Board of Directors. Vaxart’s pipeline targets coronavirus, norovirus, influenza and a therapeutic HPV indication, with vaccines designed for room-temperature storage and needle-free administration.

The appointment brings more than three decades of clinical development and regulatory experience to Vaxart’s board, including leadership roles at Oncternal Therapeutics, Bavarian Nordic, Eli Lilly and other biopharma companies, where Breitmeyer contributed to multiple FDA approvals and vaccine programs. Vaxart’s directors said his background in drug and vaccine development is expected to reinforce oversight of its clinical programs and support advancement of its oral vaccine platform as the company works to validate the benefits of its technology for patients and investors.

The most recent analyst rating on (VXRT) stock is a Buy with a $4.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Vaxart stock, see the VXRT Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on VXRT Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, VXRT is a Neutral.

The score is driven mainly by the improved TTM financial profile (rapid revenue growth and a return to profitability/positive cash flow), tempered by concerns about sustainability given historically volatile results and a smaller equity cushion. Technicals are supportive with the stock above key moving averages, while valuation looks inexpensive on P/E but lacks dividend support and relies on potentially non-recurring earnings. Earnings call factors are constructive due to partnership validation and extended runway, but remain risk-weighted due to BARDA-related delays and partner-dependency.

To see Spark’s full report on VXRT stock, click here.

More about Vaxart

Vaxart, Inc. is a clinical-stage biotechnology company developing oral recombinant vaccines using a proprietary pill-based delivery platform. Its vaccines are designed to be stored and shipped without refrigeration and target indications including coronavirus, norovirus, influenza and a therapeutic human papillomavirus vaccine, supported by broad domestic and international patent filings.

The company aims to create oral versions of currently marketed vaccines and new recombinant vaccines, positioning itself in the vaccine market as a needle-free, more easily distributed alternative. By focusing on adenovirus- and TLR3 agonist-based technologies, Vaxart is pursuing both infectious disease prevention and immune-oncology applications, with a particular emphasis on global accessibility and simplified vaccination logistics.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Current Market Cap: $182.8M

data.title: Mawson Infrastructure Appeals Nasdaq Delisting Amid Rebranding Plan
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Mawson Infrastructure Group ( (MIGI) ) has shared an announcement.

On April 17, 2026, Mawson Infrastructure Group received a Nasdaq delisting determination after its reported stockholders’ equity fell below the $2.5 million requirement as of December 31, 2025. The company intends to request a hearing before a Nasdaq panel, a move that will stay any suspension or delisting while it presents its case that it now meets the equity standard.

In a parallel step, the company is rebranding as Big Digital Energy, Inc., with the legal name change in Delaware effective April 24, 2026, and its Nasdaq ticker expected to switch from MIGI to BGDE on April 30, 2026. The rebrand, accompanied by a new corporate website, is pitched by management as a strategic pivot toward energy-focused digital infrastructure, but the pending Nasdaq review keeps regulatory and listing risks in focus for shareholders.

Chief executive Phil Stanley framed the delisting notice as a legacy issue of prior management and asserted that the company’s current financial position and operational trajectory support ongoing compliance. For investors, the combination of a fresh corporate identity, unchanged CUSIP, and an active appeal process suggests continuity of trading in the near term, while highlighting the importance of the Nasdaq panel’s eventual decision for the firm’s market standing.

Spark’s Take on MIGI Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, MIGI is a Neutral.

The score is driven down primarily by weak financial performance (persistent losses, negative equity, and renewed cash burn). Technicals provide some near-term support due to price strength versus moving averages, but overbought signals and high volatility elevate risk. Corporate events are mixed, with strategic repositioning offset by governance/legal turbulence, while valuation is constrained by negative earnings and no dividend.

To see Spark’s full report on MIGI stock, click here.

More about Mawson Infrastructure Group

Mawson Infrastructure Group Inc., set to be renamed Big Digital Energy, Inc., is a U.S.-based technology company that designs, builds, and operates next-generation digital infrastructure platforms. It provides artificial intelligence and high-performance computing services, alongside Bitcoin and other digital asset mining, through both self-mining and colocation models, emphasizing carbon-free energy sources such as nuclear power.

With 129 megawatts of capacity already online and additional capacity under development, the company positions itself as a competitive provider of carbon-aware digital infrastructure solutions. Its vertically integrated approach targets scalability and efficiency for enterprise customers that require intensive compute applications and environmentally sustainable power.

The company is currently listed on Nasdaq under the ticker MIGI, with plans to transition to BGDE as part of its broader rebranding. Its strategy focuses on serving the growing digital economy’s computing needs while seeking to maintain exchange listing compliance and access to capital markets for continued infrastructure expansion.

Average Trading Volume: 547,200

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $35.06M

Find detailed analytics on MIGI stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Customers Bancorp Posts Strong Q1 2026 Results, Growth
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Customers Bancorp ( (CUBI) ) has provided an update.

Customers Bancorp reported strong first-quarter 2026 results on April 23, 2026, with net income available to common shareholders of $69.7 million, or $1.97 per diluted share, and core earnings essentially in line. The bank delivered ROAA of 1.13%, ROCE of about 13%, an efficiency ratio below 50%, and year-over-year gains in revenue, core earnings, and book value per share of 58%, 28% and 16%, respectively.

Loans and leases held for investment rose 3.7% quarter-over-quarter and 15.3% year-over-year to $17.4 billion, driven by specialized C&I, mortgage finance, and commercial real estate segments, while investment securities decreased modestly. Deposits grew 3.9% from the prior quarter and 14.0% year-over-year to $21.6 billion, with a lower average cost and record non-interest-bearing balances, as capital and tangible common equity climbed to $2.1 billion and asset quality remained strong with NPAs at 0.29% of total assets and reserves at 337% of non-performing loans.

The quarter also marked the completion of a planned leadership transition effective January 1, 2026, with Sam Sidhu succeeding Jay Sidhu as CEO and emphasizing disciplined growth in structure and credit quality. The company increased borrowings mainly through FHLB advances, enhanced liquidity coverage of uninsured deposits to roughly 151%, improved its TCE/TA ratio to 8.3%, and repurchased over 621,000 common shares, signaling confidence in its franchise strength and balance sheet resilience.

The most recent analyst rating on (CUBI) stock is a Buy with a $84.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Customers Bancorp stock, see the CUBI Stock Forecast page.

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CUBI is a Outperform.

This is tempered by only mid-level financial statement quality driven by normalized profitability versus 2021–2022 and historical cash-flow volatility, while valuation appears reasonable on an ~11x P/E.

To see Spark’s full report on CUBI stock, click here.

Customers Bancorp, Inc., based in West Reading, Pa., operates as a commercial bank holding company focused on loans and leases to commercial and industrial clients, commercial real estate borrowers, and mortgage finance customers. The bank emphasizes specialized C&I lending, multifamily and owner-occupied CRE, and maintains a significant base of non-interest-bearing and digitally sourced deposits.

On April 23, 2026, JPMorgan Chase & Co. closed a series of registered public debt offerings totaling $10 billion across multiple tranches of floating rate and fixed-to-floating rate notes maturing between 2030 and 2037. By tapping investors for both shorter- and longer-dated instruments, the firm diversified its liability structure and appealed to demand for interest rate–sensitive products. data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

Ameris Bancorp ( (ABCB) ) has shared an announcement.

Ameris Bancorp reported a strong first quarter of 2026, with net income rising to $110.5 million, or $1.63 per diluted share, for the period ended March 31, 2026, up from $87.9 million a year earlier, driven by higher net interest income and robust noninterest revenue. Return on average assets reached 1.62%, return on average tangible common equity climbed to 14.75% and net interest margin expanded to 3.88%, underscoring outperformance versus much of the regional banking sector.

Earning assets grew by $607.8 million on an annualized basis, supported by $314.5 million in loan growth and $260.7 million in deposit growth, while the noninterest-bearing deposit mix improved to 29.8% of total deposits, helping reduce the total cost of funds to 1.88%. The efficiency ratio improved year over year to 49.97% despite seasonal expense pressures, credit quality remained solid with annualized net charge-offs of 0.21% and stable reserves and the company returned capital by repurchasing about $74.9 million of stock, or roughly 1.4% of outstanding equity, while growing tangible book value per share to $44.79.

Noninterest income increased 13.1% from the prior quarter to $69.9 million, with mortgage banking revenue up 16.1% and other fee income helped by the absence of prior mortgage servicing right losses and higher gains on SBA loan sales. Total assets reached $28.11 billion, shareholders’ equity stood at $4.08 billion and tangible common equity represented 11.15% of tangible assets, positioning Ameris with a stronger balance sheet and capital base as it continues to expand in its Southeast markets.

Happy City Holdings Limited Class A ( (HCHL) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On April 21, 2026, Happy City Holdings Limited signed a Standby Equity Purchase Agreement with YA II PN, Ltd., giving the company the right, but not the obligation, to sell up to $20 million of its Class A ordinary shares over 36 months. The purchase price per share will be based on the lowest daily volume‑weighted average price over three trading days following each drawdown notice, with no mandatory minimum advances or non‑usage fees.

Happy City has already paid a $25,000 structuring fee and issued 72,401 Class A shares as half of the commitment fee, with the remaining $100,000 to be paid in cash at the first advance closing. Share sales under the facility can only begin once a resale registration statement becomes effective, positioning the company with flexible, on‑demand equity financing that may support future capital needs while subject to ownership and registration limits designed to manage dilution and regulatory compliance.
